div.head: The Minister in Colombia (Caffery) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 28. My telegram number 27, February 19, 7 p.m. Monday last, Minister for Foreign Affairs showed me Olaya’s latest telegram saying he had signed note and had sent copies regulations by air mail. I asked him what steps Pan American should take in order to begin service. He replied of course he did not know exactly what regulations stipulated but that all he now wanted was formal notice through the Legation that Pan American desired to initiate service; he thought it would be well to include usual data about machines and names of pilots.
However, Doctor Uribe believes this to be the reciprocal procedure stipulated in the regulations.
